summary: eth-wallet-sentinel exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package executes a system-information exfiltration payload at install time and from scanner.js. package.json line 30 contains an inline Node.js script that requires child_process, os, and https, collects hostname, id, and process.env, and POSTs the data to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js reinforces the same fingerprint: it imports fs/os/https/child_process, calls os.hostname() and execSync('whoami') to build a host profile, walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync, and shells out via curl to send the collected data over https.request/https.get to https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced at lines 161 and 323). The destination is a github.io pages site — a static-hosting endpoint with no relationship to anything an Ethereum wallet utility would legitimately need to talk to, and the repository owner ddjidd564 has no apparent vendor identity. The combination of install-time process.env exfiltration, a hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 URL, and a sibling file (scanner.js) that performs deeper host enumeration and curl-based exfiltration to the same endpoint is an unambiguous credential/host-info stealer. Installing this package leaks environment variables (which commonly include API keys, cloud credentials, and CI tokens), the machine hostname, the current user, and filesystem reconnaissance results to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
